summary: Round-2 re-review. All four round-1 findings (1 major + 3 minor) are correctly resolved. The major pairing-coverage gap is genuinely fixed: prev now lists slots in the OPPOSITE row order from curr ([logical_a, physical_b] vs [physical_b, logical_a]). Verified against internal/stat/postgres.go: diff() pairs rows by cv[ukey]==pv[ukey] (slot_name, ukey=0) and diffPair computes raw cv-pv with no clamping, so positional pairing would diff physical_b(0) against logical_a(10..80) yielding negative deltas — the golden and the exact-delta Contains assertions would change. The slot_name pairing property is now actually exercised, not merely asserted. Minor #2: independent value-level guards added (stripANSI + whitespace collapse, then assert.Contains on the full computed delta rows for logical_a '5 8 15 12 20 30 30 50' and physical_b all-zero) — diff math is now pinned independent of the golden. Minor #3: assert.NotContains(out, ', rate: ') added as the primary no-rows signal in the empty case. Minor #4: empty-case comment tightened to state doReport (not RunMain) is under test, scoping the INFO/WARNING guards to procpidstat-only diagnostics. Both tests pass (go test ./report/... -run ReplSlots is green); golden sha256 9d59a11a... confirms regenerated output is byte-identical, proving pairing is by key.
